title: penstemon humilis subsp. obtusifolius
intro: Penstemon humilis subsp. obtusifolius, also known as the dwarf or obtuse-leaved beardtongue, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is characterized by its small, pink to purple flowers and distinctive beard-like structure on its lower lip. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Penstemon humilis subsp. obtusifolius can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, and it is also used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic and antiviral properties.
